data_in.description: GNPS mass spectral molecular network file (graphml or cytoscape format)
Chemical class metadata or library mapping

data_out.description: Chemically-annotated GNPS molecular network file with chemical class information mapped to network nodes

domain_knowledge: Chemical class annotation in GNPS networks uses compound structural classification systems (e.g., NPClassifier or ClassyFire hierarchies) to organize spectral nodes by chemical family.
pyMolNetEnhancer integrates chemical class data as node attributes and edge metadata, enabling visual and computational filtering by compound type within the molecular network.
GNPS networks are typically exported in graphml or cytoscape-compatible formats; chemical class mapping preserves network topology while adding semantic chemical information.

Chemical class information may be sourced from GNPS library matches, external databases, or pre-computed predictions; consistent identifier matching between network nodes and class metadata is critical for accurate mapping.

direct_checks: verify file exists: pyMolNetEnhancer package accessible from github:madeleineernst__pyMolNetEnhancer
script_runs: pyMolNetEnhancer chemical class mapping function executes on a GNPS network JSON or GraphML input file without raising exceptions
file_format_is: output annotated network file is valid GraphML or JSON format (robust to GraphML/JSON variant specifications)
file_exists: output file contains at least one node with chemical class attribute added relative to input network
field_present: output network nodes include chemical class label field(s)
contains_substring: output network artifact contains evidence of class-to-node mapping (e.g., class field values populated, not null or empty)
expert_review: chemical class assignments are biochemically sensible for the compound nodes in the test network
chemical class mapping preserves the original GNPS molecular network structure and edge connectivity
annotation coverage and accuracy of chemical class assignments match expectations for the input dataset

workflow_description: 1. Load a GNPS mass spectral molecular network file (e.g., graphml or cytoscape format). 2. Prepare chemical class metadata or retrieve it from the GNPS public library. 3. Use pyMolNetEnhancer to map chemical class information onto nodes and edges of the molecular network. 4. Export the annotated network in a standard format (e.g., graphml, cytoscape) with chemical class labels and attributes embedded.
